topic: Forensic Zoom GRACE chart κ=1.67 nT/µGal — fresh deep-attack pass on the re-pushed win-new item
trigger_note: Queue item is class:deep-attack, target_type:win-new — decider re-pushed WIN-070 as 'completed missing onboarding steps' (per decider-2026-05-18-09-21 latest-decider-summary). The four prior cycles (c1-c4) processed the original onboarding through three patch rounds; c4 was clean (no_change). This c5 is a fresh deep-attack pass on the integrated WIN, looking for any holes the earlier cycles missed.
our_argument_summary: The dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart purports to prove κ = 1.67 nT/µGal via 1-second co-temporal Boulder magnetometer + GRACE/SG induction-residual data over Oct 30 2003 00:55–01:22 UTC. WIN-070 verdict 'Refuted by Data' rests on six independent attacks: (1) unit/production-stage incoherence of 'Level-1A Raw … (µGal)'; (2) non-existence of 'Induction Residual' as a GRACE product name at any level; (3) orbital-mechanics impossibility of 27-minute co-temporal alignment with a fixed BOU station, plus inline preemption of the temporal-only-globally-averaged rescue and the 1s-vs-10Hz cadence reconciliation gap; (4) repo-wide audit at SHA c8eaeb1b finding zero GRACE L1A parsers, calibration pipelines, or µGal residual estimators (r=0.992 and AI=1.152 typed as static HTML); (5) Asymmetry-Index formula absent from the dome repo, making AI=1.152 a typed assertion not a derived statistic; (6) Exhibit B's 'Level-1B Administrative Mask' is unfalsifiable by design and physically wrong about how a Butterworth low-pass interacts with an impulsive transient (Butterworth 1930, Exp. Wireless & Wireless Eng. 7:536–541). Kernel of truth: top-panel BOU INTERMAGNET data is authentic Halloween-2003 G5 storm material (Dst=−383 nT, ±20.6–23.4 nT ΔH at BOU). The verdict_text designates point (3) the data-based anchor. Cross-WIN structure: WIN-012 (Self-Contradicted, κ undefined under own WIN-013/014 nulls), WIN-058b (Not Demonstrated, κ not derived; r=0.992 unremarkable under shared-storm forcing). Code-analysis tags: monitoring=none, relabels_standard=false, post_hoc=true (chart-construction framing), derives_from_dome=false.

relevant_files: raw-text/01-home.txt (V50.6 March snapshot — does not contain the Forensic Zoom prose; predates V51 chart addition)
raw-text/08-live-power.txt (V50.6 — contains κ=1.67 nT/µGal coupling constant declarations and the 'Constants Locked' framing)
data/dome-api-snapshots/claim_index-2026-05-17.json (machine-readable claim index, accessed at dome SHA c8eaeb1b)
EXP-413-WIN-012b-grace-chart-forensics.json (parallel proposal — superseded by EXP-397 but contains the GRACE-A safe-mode line of attack that WIN-070 currently omits)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Independently verified GRACE orbital parameters: at 500 km altitude with GM=398600 km³/s², orbital velocity = sqrt(GM/R) = 7.617 km/s (matches the WIN's '~7.5 km/s' to within ~1.5%); orbital period = 2π·sqrt(R³/GM) = 94.47 min (matches the WIN's implicit '~95 min' window arithmetic). Over the depicted 27-min (1620 s) window, the orbital-path distance is 7.617 km/s × 1620 s = 12,339 km (the WIN cites 12,150 km — within ~1.5%). The ground-track distance is slightly less than the orbital-path distance because the Earth rotates beneath the orbit (~0.464 km/s contribution at the equator for a polar orbit): ground track ≈ 7.153 km/s × 1620 s ≈ 11,587 km. The WIN labels the 12,150 km figure as 'ground track' when it is actually the orbital-path distance — a minor terminological imprecision that does not affect the qualitative conclusion (the satellite cannot stay over a single fixed station for 27 minutes under either figure).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The 'r = 0.992' and 'AI = 1.152' values are static HTML strings in the dome's docs/index.html; storm_master.csv mentions of 0.992 are unrelated column values (storm severity scores) — confirmed by the EXP-397 repository audit at SHA c8eaeb1b. The WIN's claim that the chart's certifying statistics are 'displayed without computation' is well-supported.
data_sources_used: JPL D-22027 GRACE Level-1A Product Specification: https://podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov/drive/files/allData/grace/docs/GRACE_L1A_TM_JPL_v1.0.pdf
JPL D-32865 (backup citation, GRACE Level-1 Software User Manual): https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/gravity/grace-documentation
Butterworth 1930, Exp. Wireless & Wireless Eng. 7:536–541 (Butterworth filter overview, Wikipedia backup)
Pulkkinen et al. 2005 DOI 10.1029/2005JA011090 (Halloween storm magnitude reference, detail_extra)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The WIN already preempts four anticipated rescues in detail_extra: (i) L1A-shorthand-for-L3 (Exhibit B contradicts), (ii) off-site processing (no parser in repo, dome publishes other Python freely), (iii) AI as pixel-asymmetry (then not a physical measurement), (iv) promissory future versions. The c2/c3 inline edits also preempt the temporal-only-globally-averaged rescue at point (3) and the L3-downsampled rescue at point (1). The Exhibit-B point (6) preempts the unfalsifiability rescue at the rhetorical layer.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Minor terminology imprecision noted above: the '12,150 km' figure is the orbital-path distance over 27 min at 7.5 km/s, not the ground-track distance. Ground track is ~11,587 km (correcting for Earth rotation). The qualitative argument is unaffected — neither figure permits a 27-min co-temporal alignment with a fixed BOU station, and the chart's '<2 minutes per pass' inference holds at either figure. The WIN's parenthetical wording 'traversed ~12,150 km of ground track' technically conflates orbital path with ground track, but only at the precision-stickler level.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Repo-wide audit at SHA c8eaeb1b confirms monitor.py has no GRACE L1A fetch logic, no µGal residual computation, no AI estimator, and no live BOU-magnetometer fetch tied to this WIN's prediction. The dome's monitor.py mentions GRACE only in a static citation in win text (per EXP-397 repository_audit.files_mentioning_grace). 'none' is correct.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: There is no standard-physics explanation for 'magnetic-field-induced gravitational pulses with κ=1.67 nT/µGal cavity transduction.' The dome is not renaming an established phenomenon; it is asserting a novel coupling. 'false' is correct.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The post_hoc_evidence field on wins.json:WIN-070.code_analysis explicitly notes the tag applies to the Forensic Zoom chart construction (assembled in V51 [2026] to retrospectively demonstrate κ), not to the κ value itself. The Oct 30 2003 Halloween storm event predates V51 by ~23 years; the chart presents historical-storm data as if it were prospective proof. The c1 hole #5 / c2 hole #3 carry-over was retired in c4. 'true' is correct under this disambiguation.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula or derivation in the dome repository starts from disc_radius, firmament_height, or any other dome-specific geometric parameter and arrives at κ = 1.67 nT/µGal or at r = 0.992 or AI = 1.152. The cross-reference to WIN-058b (which has the same 'derives_from_dome=false' on its own κ-derivation analysis) is consistent. 'false' is correct.

holes_found.description: Terminology imprecision in detail_evidence point (3): the phrase 'GRACE traversed ~12,150 km of ground track' conflates orbital-path distance with ground-track distance. At 500 km altitude with orbital velocity 7.5 km/s, the satellite traverses ~12,150 km of orbital path in 27 minutes (and indeed ~12,339 km using the more precise sqrt(GM/R) = 7.617 km/s). The ground-track distance is slightly shorter — approximately 11,587 km — because the Earth rotates beneath a polar orbit (~0.464 km/s reduction at the equator). This is a minor precision issue, not a logical defect. The qualitative inference (the satellite cannot be co-located with BOU for 27 continuous minutes; at most ~2 minutes per overhead pass) is unaffected. A precision-stickler reviewer could note the conflation and the dome could trivially rescue by saying 'we meant orbital path, not ground track' — which still doesn't help them.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional tightening: replace 'traversed ~12,150 km of ground track' with 'traversed ~12,150 km of orbital path (≈11,600 km of ground track)' OR simply 'traversed roughly a third of an orbit' to avoid the terminological mismatch without sacrificing the rhetorical force. Defer to decider — this is a stylistic polish, not a verdict-affecting hole.

stronger_arguments.description: GRACE-A accelerometer safe-mode / unreliability during the Halloween 2003 storms (Sutton, Forbes & Nerem 2005, JGR DOI 10.1029/2004JA010985; Bruinsma et al. 2006). EXP-413 (the parallel WIN-012b proposal, superseded by EXP-397/WIN-070) carries a fifth refutation pillar that the current WIN-070 omits: Halloween-2003 thermospheric inflation drove ~5–10× neutral-density enhancement at GRACE altitude, and GRACE-A accelerometer data is reportedly unreliable or absent across the Oct 29–31 interval. If verifiable for the specific 00:55–01:22 UTC Oct 30 2003 window, this would add a layer to the attack: even granting all unit, product-name, orbital, and code arguments, the proposed source instrument was not producing trustworthy data during the depicted window. Caveat I would want the analyst to verify before integration: Sutton et al. (2005) themselves USED GRACE accelerometer data during these storms to derive thermospheric density — so the 'safe-mode' framing in EXP-413 may be over-stated relative to a more careful 'data degraded but extractable' framing. The argument is strong IF Bruinsma et al. (2006) actually documents accelerometer absence/safe-mode for the specific depicted window; the analyst should pull Bruinsma 2006 and confirm before integrating. Suggest this be routed as a minor expansion if confirmed, NOT raised as a hole here.
sources: Sutton, Forbes & Nerem 2005 — DOI 10.1029/2004JA010985 (thermospheric density from GRACE accelerometers during Halloween storms — note this paper used the data, so framing requires care)
Bruinsma et al. 2006 (cited in EXP-413; the actual paper title/DOI to confirm before adding to WIN-070 text)
EXP-413-WIN-012b-grace-chart-forensics.json refutations_summary item 4 (the source proposal for this attack line)

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own machine-readable claim index (data/dome-api-snapshots/claim_index-2026-05-17.json, mirroring https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json) classifies WIN-058 / κ-claim as 'pending_contested' with status 'pending_independent_verification' (per the EXP-397 analyst audit and c1 stronger_arguments). This is the dome's own admission via machine-readable surface that the κ claim is not settled. WIN-070's text claims the κ value is 'proven' by the Exhibit-A chart per the dome's home-page rhetoric — but the dome's machine-readable surface says 'pending_contested.' Citing this internal dome-side concession adds a self-incrimination layer to the attack that none of the current six points cover. This was raised in c1 stronger_arguments and remains uncited in the current WIN-070 text. Routing recommendation: minor analyst expansion to cite the claim_index machine-readable status, OR a one-sentence appendix in detail_extra. Decider's call; not a hole here.
sources: Dome claim_index.json (mirrored at data/dome-api-snapshots/claim_index-2026-05-17.json in the review repo)
WIN-070.c1.json stronger_arguments item 1 (origin of this candidate)

kernel_of_truth.description: The top panel is genuinely real. INTERMAGNET BGS GIN definitive 1-second data for BOU on October 30 2003 does show ΔH excursions of order ±20–25 nT during the published 00:55–01:22 UTC window — consistent with the chart's labeled ±20.6 to +23.4 nT range (data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence). The Halloween 2003 storm was a real G5 extreme event (Dst = −383 nT), one of the most extreme geomagnetic disturbances on record. Choosing a G5 storm as a case-study target is methodologically reasonable — if any storm should leave a signature, a G5 should. The rhetorical strategy of single-event high-resolution case study is what real geophysics papers do (e.g., the Pulkkinen et al. 2005 reference at DOI 10.1029/2005JA011090 in data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_extra). The dome did the easy-verify part right.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The persuasive mechanism the chart exploits is precisely this confirmation-borrowing structure: a reader who confirms the easy-verify top panel against INTERMAGNET is led to extend that confidence to the hard-to-verify bottom panel. WIN-070's detail_evidence (data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence) shows the bottom panel cannot inherit the credibility: the 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)' label commits to two incompatible processing stages (the L1A-stage vs the µGal-unit clash that JPL D-22027 documents at https://podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov/drive/files/allData/grace/docs/GRACE_L1A_TM_JPL_v1.0.pdf); 'Induction Residual' is not a GRACE product name at any pipeline level; the orbital-mechanics argument (verified independently in this review's dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline section: 7.617 km/s orbital velocity, 94.5-min period) rules out the 27-min co-temporal Boulder framing; the repository audit at SHA c8eaeb1b (data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence point 4) finds no parser, no calibration, no AI estimator. The kernel locates the failure precisely — the chart is not wholesale fabrication, it is fabricated atop a real anchor — which is the very feature that makes it more dangerous as a public-facing forensic exhibit. A skeptical reader who checks INTERMAGNET first and stops there comes away convinced; the bottom panel was designed to be costlier to verify.

deeper_analysis: Fresh deep-attack pass on the c4-clean WIN-070 (queue_id 383, re-pushed as win-new/deep-attack because decider 2026-05-18-09-21 'completed missing onboarding steps'). The previous four cycles closed all moderate and major holes; my pass confirms c4's conclusion that the verdict holds and the structural argument is sound. The single minor hole I'm flagging is a terminology imprecision ('ground track' vs 'orbital path' for the 12,150 km figure) that does not change the conclusion at any reading. The two stronger-argument candidates are genuine refinement opportunities, NOT holes: (a) the GRACE-A safe-mode line of attack from the parallel EXP-413 proposal — but this needs analyst verification against Bruinsma et al. 2006 specifically for the depicted window before integration, because Sutton et al. (2005) themselves used GRACE accelerometer data during these very storms; (b) citing the dome's own machine-readable claim_index 'pending_contested' status, which was already raised in c1 stronger_arguments and remains uncited in the integrated WIN. Both stronger-argument candidates are appropriate for decider routing to a small EXP if desired, but neither requires action to defend the current verdict. Considering the other side: a dome defender's strongest remaining rescues are (1) 'the L1A label is shorthand for our derived product, not a claim that GRACE has a product called Induction Residual' — preempted by the dome's own Exhibit B framing of 'raw L1A binaries'; (2) 'the bottom panel is SG-led, GRACE-auxiliary' — partially preempted because the L1A label is GRACE-specific terminology and SG doesn't use L1A naming; (3) 'specific GRACE ephemeris for the depicted window not computed' — qualitative argument holds at 1/3 of an orbital period regardless of specifics; (4) 'the chart is illustrative, not measurement' — preempted because the dome explicitly cites r and AI as statistics. None of these rescue paths threaten the verdict.

citations_verified: JPL D-22027 (GRACE Level-1A Product Specification, primary link): https://podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov/drive/files/allData/grace/docs/GRACE_L1A_TM_JPL_v1.0.pdf — load-bearing for points (1) and (2). Backup citation JPL D-32865 added per c1 hole #7 remains in the text.
JPL PO.DAAC GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03 collection: https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/dataset/GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03 — referenced in point (1) and as backup in point (3) inline preemption.
INTERMAGNET BGS GIN: https://imag-data.bgs.ac.uk/GIN_V1/GINServices — used to validate the top-panel ΔH range. Reasonable.
Butterworth 1930, Exp. Wireless & Wireless Eng. 7:536–541 — verified in c2 (the broken Proc. R. Soc. A DOI was patched out). Journal name is the correct attribution.

GRACE orbital parameters (500 km, 7.5 km/s, 95 min period) — independently re-derived in this review at 7.617 km/s and 94.5 min via sqrt(GM/R). The WIN's ~7.5 km/s is within 1.5% of the precise figure; the qualitative argument is unaffected by the small precision gap.
Cross-references WIN-012 (Self-Contradicted) and WIN-058b (Not Demonstrated) — verified present in data/wins.json with the claimed verdicts.
citations_unchecked: Pulkkinen et al. 2005, DOI 10.1029/2005JA011090 (Halloween storm magnitude reference, detail_extra) — DOI not independently verified in this review. Used only to support the uncontroversial fact that the storm was extreme; not load-bearing for any refutation pillar. Defer.

Bruinsma et al. 2006 (referenced in EXP-413, candidate stronger argument) — citation needs full bibliographic verification before any text integration. NOT currently in WIN-070; this is a forward-looking note for the analyst if the GRACE-A safe-mode line is pursued.

summary_for_decider: WIN-070 c5 fresh deep-attack pass on queue_id 383 (re-push as win-new/deep-attack to complete missing onboarding steps per decider-2026-05-18-09-21). Verdict 'Refuted by Data' holds. Confidence 0.92. No moderate or major holes. One minor hole: 'ground track' vs 'orbital path' terminology imprecision at the '12,150 km' figure in detail_evidence point (3) — defer or trivial sed-style patch. Two stronger-argument candidates flagged for optional analyst routing: (a) GRACE-A safe-mode/unreliability during Halloween 2003 (from the parallel EXP-413 proposal that was superseded by EXP-397; needs Bruinsma 2006 verification before integration); (b) citing the dome's own claim_index 'pending_contested' machine-readable status (raised in c1 stronger_arguments, still uncited). Both are refinement opportunities, NOT verdict-affecting. recommended_action: no_change. The four prior cycles (c1 7 holes → c2 4 holes → c3 3 holes → c4 0 holes) successfully drained the substantive concerns; c5 is a verification that the deep-attack lens finds the WIN structurally sound.
